data.title: Gold nears another big milestone in final stretch of the week
data.description:
Gotta bring this out from the archives:

Gold had a bit of a rough time at the end of last month when broader markets in general were seeing corrective moves. But once again, the recovery has been nothing short of outstanding. Dip buyers were quick to arrest the shift in sentiment and we're back to more bullish talk for gold.

Gold (XAU/USD) daily chart

Trade conflict and risk aversion continues to add to more factors that should benefit the precious metal. And that seems to be how things are playing out, a little too straightforward it would seem.

With major central banks still set to cut rates further and also still stocking up on gold supply, it's hard to fight the bigger picture narrative in gold. However, this run higher since last year has been relentless. There's really no point in which to catch a breath and now we're closing in on the $3,000 mark just three months into the new year.

The major concern for me when it comes to gold is that we're still yet to see a healthy correction since the break above key resistance above $2,075 last year. That's a whopping ~45% gains without much of a check back. It does feel overdue to some extent. So as we approach another big milestone, are we going to see buyers engage in profit-taking? Or is this going to be just another blast through to the next leg higher?

data.title: Muthoot Finance's Gold Loan Assets Under Management Top INR1 Trillion
data.description: Muthoot Finance said its gold loan assets under management have crossed the 1 trillion-Indian-rupee mark as of March 13, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

data.title: Market Chatter: BP to Market 50% Strategic Stake in Solar Unit
data.description:
BP (BP.L) is gearing up to sell half of its solar unit for cash and a commitment for future investments, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a March sales document to had seen.

The British oil and gas company will accept initial and non-binding proposals for Lightsource bp until June as part of the sales process for the so-called project Scala. The first screening of the bidders is expected in July.

The document specified that BP is looking to partner with "established leaders with extensive experience" in the renewables industry, the report added. BP told Reuters the sale process will be launched soon.

BP did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: China's Consumption and Investment Likely Accelerated in January-February, Poll Says — Market Talk
data.description: China's retail sales, a proxy for consumption and fixed-asset investment likely accelerated in the January-February period, despite rising trade tensions with the U.S., according to 20 economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. China combines January and February data to iron out distortions brought on by the shifting time of the Lunar New Year holiday. Retail sales likely expanded 4% on year in the first two months, up from December's 3.7%. Meanwhile, fixed-asset investment is expected to have risen 3.5% year on year in January-February, compared with 2024's 3.2%. Growth in industrial production likely slowed to 5.4% during the January-February period from December's 6.2%, in part due to high-base effects. China's statistics bureau will release the data on Monday. (singaporeeditors@dowjones.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: MGI Tech Sees Demand Surge After China Bans US Genome Sequencing Products
data.description:
MGI Tech , a Chinese manufacturer of genome sequencing machines, said it is experiencing a significant surge in demand following China's import ban on similar products from US-based companies like Illumina, Yicai Global reported Thursday.

The ban, implemented by China's Ministry of Commerce on Feb. 4, citing national security concerns and placing Illumina on its "unreliable entity list," has prompted domestic customers to seek alternative suppliers, the report said.

MGI Tech is poised to be the primary beneficiary of this market shift, as Illumina's substantial market share is redistributed among local competitors, according to a research report by Guosen Securities.

Prior to the ban, Illumina held a significant presence in the Chinese market, accounting for approximately 26.5% of new gene-sequencing machine installations, generating revenues of $310 million in the past year, the report said.

This market share is now expected to be largely captured by MGI Tech and other domestic manufacturers, Yicai reported.

MGI's shares slipped 1% in recent trade.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: RBA Has Chance to Comment on Trade War, Plunge in Markets — Market Talk
data.description: A speech on Tuesday by Sarah Hunter, the Reserve Bank of Australia's chief economic forecaster, could be the first opportunity for the central bank to comment on recent upheavals in the global economy. So far there's no topic for the speech which will be delivered to the AFR Banking Summit. Still, it's likely that Hunter, who comes from a markets background, will be asked about the potential for the trade war and falling share markets to change the outlook for world growth, something that is critical to Australia, a massive exporter of commodities to the global economy. While the direct impact of tariffs on Australia is small, it could feel a lot of pain if China slow and investor confidence continues to crumble. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

data.title: Yunnan Copper to Form Copper Mining Joint Venture
data.description:
Yunnan Copper is planning to form a joint venture with Liangshan Industrial Investment to establish a subsidiary in Liangshan Prefecture in China's Sichuan Province.

The new company has a registered capital of 500 million yuan, with Yunnan Copper investing 300 million yuan for a 60% stake and Liangshan Industrial Investment investing 200 million yuan for a 40% stake, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The joint venture will focus on copper mining, smelting and processing.

Yunnan Copper shares rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Sinocare's Vice Chairman Raises Stake for 1.5 Million Yuan
data.description:
Sinocare's vice chairman raised holdings in the company for a total of 1.5 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

Li Xinyi, the company's vice chairman, will increase her holdings from 753,200 shares to 815,800 shares, raising her stake from 0.13% to 0.14%.

Li is also the daughter of the chairman, Li Shaobo, the blood glucose monitor manufacturer said.

Sinocare's shares rose more than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: HY Electronic (Cayman) Decides Against Paying Dividend for 2024
data.description:
HY Electronic (Cayman) will not distribute dividends for 2024 following a board resolution, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The high-power semiconductor components manufacturer did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: Strickland Metals Swings to Loss in Fiscal H1; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Strickland Metals reported Thursday a fiscal half-year loss of AU$0.0014 per diluted share, compared with earnings of AU$0.0165 per diluted share a year earlier.

The mining firm posted a AU$1.1 million gain on net disposal of financial assets which was not present in the previous half-year.

Conversely, the previous half-year period included a AU$31.6 million net profit from discontinued operations not reflected in the reported half-year.

Strickland Metals' shares were up 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Ayala: Results Supported by Strong Growth Across All Businesses Including Banking, Property >AC.PH
Ayala Corp. 2024 Net PHP42.03B Vs. Net PHP38.07B >AC.PH
Trendzon Holdings Raises HK$40 Million Through Private Bond Placement
data.description:
Trendzon Holdings Group has raised HK$40 million through a private placement of bonds to a single investor, Central Point Holding Development, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The bonds, issued on March 13, carry a 3% annual interest rate and will mature in 2026. Cheer Union Securities acted as the agent placing the order. The bonds are unsecured and non-listed, with an option for early redemption.

The funds will support the company's financial strategy and business development, Trendzon said.

The company's shares were down by almost 14% in recent trading.

data.title: Jollibee Foods Sees 2025 Capex PHP18.0B-PHP21.0B >JFC.PH
Jollibee Foods Plans to Open 700-800 Stores Across Regions in 2025>JFC.PH
Forexlive Asia-pacific FX news wrap: Optimism creeps back in as Washington turmoil ebbs
data.description:
Key points:
  • Senate Democrats back off on government shutdown threats
  • Schumer confirms that Democrats will vote to keep government open
  • Ontario premier: We had a very productive meeting, will have another next week
  • Canada finance minister: We agreed to maintain dialogue
  • Lutnick releases statement after meeting Canadian officials (spoiler, it doesn't say much)
  • Bessent: Hopefully we don't get a recursive Biden-flation
  • EU's Kallas: It's most likely that Russia will agree to ceasefire with conditions
  • Jeff Gundlach sees a 60% chance of a US recession this year, sees gold hitting $4000
  • Fox Business reporter sends mixed messages on hopes for Mexico-Canada tariff relief
  • New Zealand February food price index -0.5% vs +1.9% prior
  • New Zealand manufacturing PMI 53.9 vs 51.4 prior

Markets:

  • WTI crude oil up $0.45 to $67.00
  • US 10-year yields up 2 bps to 4.29%
  • S&P 500 futures up 0.8%
  • Hang Seng +2.4%
  • Nikkei +0.8%
  • NZD leads, JPY lags

It's a nice risk-on day to wrap up the Asian trading week and there aren't really any caveats in the price action as all the risk-positive moves are unfolding in the textbook way.

There were two catalysts, the first was a decision by Senate Democrats to vote for the Republican bill and avoid a government shutdown. The second was a meeting between US and Canadian officials where the Canadian side left feeling upbeat (and the US side stayed quiet). It wasn't exactly a rousing success but ti came with other indications that some people in Republican circles have had enough of the trade war.

There are also those looking to buy dips in risk assets and some real strength in China today is adding to the enthusiasm. So far, the moves in FX are modest outside some decent yen selling. Headlines out of Tokyo so far today have been minimal.

The other spot everyone is watching is gold. Futures have already crested $3000 for the first time and cash is now trading just $8 away with some momentum in the past few minutes following a $58 rise on Thursday. It's been a great year so far for the gold bugs as the US dollar sags and tariff worries mount.

The problem here is that you can't have gold rising on tariff worries rising and stocks climbing on tariff worries receding. Eventually that will have to unwind but at the momentum, $3000 is such a magnet.

Have a great weekend, Eamonn will be back to start the new week.

data.title: Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 18 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 3 cents. The mean expectation of three analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 7 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from -6 cents to 20 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 11.6% to $147.17 million from a year ago; analysts expected $124.18 million.

  • Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd's reported EPS for the quarter was 18 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $8.44 million.

  • Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd shares had fallen by 9.0% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 57.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no earnings estimate revisions by analysts covering the company.

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the marine freight & logistics peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Pangaea Logistics Solutions Ltd is $10.25

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data March 14 at 04:12 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Jollibee Foods: 2025 Operating Profit Growth Likely in Range of 10%-15% >JFC.PH
Jincheng Pharmaceutical's Unit Gets Nod to Trial Minocycline Hydrochloride; Shares Up 3%
data.description:
Shandong Jincheng Pharmaceutical Group's unit, Guangdong Jincheng Jinsu Pharmaceutical, will conduct clinical trials on the injectable minocycline hydrochloride after receiving approval from China's National Medical Products Administration, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Thursday.

The drug will be tested as a treatment for infections caused by specified bacterial sensitive strains, such as Rocky Mountain spotted fever, typhus, Q fever, tick-borne fever caused by Rickettsia, and other bacteria, the pharmaceutical company said.

The company's shares rose 3% in recent trade.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Financial Services News of the Day - Friday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Trump Family Has Held Deal Talks With Binance Following Crypto Exchange's Guilty Plea

The discussions for a stake in Binance's U.S. arm come as founder Changpeng Zhao has pushed for the administration to pardon him for his own 2023 guilty plea.

Victor Dodig to Retire as CEO of Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce After 11 Years

CIBC's Dodig will remain on the board as a special adviser to incoming CEO Harry Culham and the board until April 30, 2026. Culiham is to take the CEO position on Nov. 1.

Deutsche Bank Expects to Moderate Pace of Dividend Hikes Beyond 2025

The lender reaffirmed its target for capital returns to shareholders for the five-year period ending 2025, but said dividends beyond then will grow more moderately.

Tariff Threats May Slow Private Equity's Nearshoring Bets in Mexico

Uncertainty over new Trump administration trade policies force some investors to rethink Mexican deals.

How Medicaid Spending Reductions Affect Startups

Reduced federal spending would likely sharpen focus on technology to improve efficiency, potentially benefiting some venture-backed companies.

Generali Operating Profit Rises on Higher Premiums

Assicurazioni Generali said its operating profit rose on the performance of all its units over 2024 gross written premiums that increased more than expected.

Is Trump Taking a 'Liquidationist' Approach to the Economy?

The administration's view that damaging the economy now could help it later comes with little upside for investors.

Venture Investors Hoped for an IPO Recovery in 2025. Now It's On Hold.

Market volatility makes IPO pricing a "monumental challenge." Meanwhile, limited partners are under pressure as distributions dry up.

Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on Generali, Bridgepoint, Westpac and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

Banner Ridge Seeks $3.2 Billion for New Secondary Fund

The special situations and distressed-debt specialist aims to raise one of the largest vehicles to acquire secondhand private-credit fund stakes.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Friday at 12 AM ET
data.description:
Oil prices settle lower as IEA warns that trade tensions will undercut demand

Oil futures settled lower on Thursday after the International Energy Agency's monthly report underscored worries that rising global trade tensions could undercut energy demand.

U.S. Natural Gas Lifted By Above-Estimate Storage Draw

U.S. natural gas futures reversed early losses after the EIA reported a bigger-than-expected reduction in storage as the winter withdrawal season enters its final weeks.

IEA Says Trade Tensions Weigh on Oil Demand, Warns of Supply Surplus

The International Energy Agency said the macroeconomic conditions underpinning its oil-demand projections have worsened over the past month due to global trade tensions.

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Rise For Second Straight Week

U.S. crude oil inventories increased by 1.4 million barrels, with an increase in production and net imports, while gasoline stocks fell more than expected as demand picked up.

OPEC Sticks to Oil-Demand View Ahead of Output Hike

The cartel still expects demand to grow by 1.45 million barrels a day this year and 1.43 million barrels a day the next, bolstered by strong air travel demand and road mobility.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Find insight on oil futures, Contact Energy, Harbour Energy and more in the latest Market Talks covering Energy and Utilities.

Wind and Solar Overtake Coal Power for First Time in U.S.

Natural gas remains by far the largest source of electricity in the country, according to research from clean-energy think tank Ember.

Trump Pushes a Controversial Pipeline. CEO Won't Build It Without State Support.

The Williams Cos. CEO said on Tuesday that he isn't willing to undertake the project as long as the region's governors oppose it.

Clean-Energy Stocks Are Jumping as S&P 500 Dropped

Stocks of Enphase Energy, AES, and Nextera Energy gained ground. The industry has lagged behind the market for the past two years.

Energy Chief Pushes for Natural Gas Pipelines, Exports. Oil Slumps on Tariff Threat.

Chris Wright also signed a permit extension for a natural gas export terminal in Louisiana.

data.title: Global Commodities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Commodities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0326 GMT - U.S. steel and aluminum tariffs affect just 0.2% of Australia's goods exports, and proposed tariffs on pharmaceuticals will likely have a similarly small impact, says Adam Boyton, head of Australian economics at ANZ. Australia's trade deficit with the U.S. makes it an unlikely target for country-specific tariffs, he adds. Still, Australia's exposure to developments in the U.S. isn't necessarily through direct or indirect trade linkages. Rather, it is more likely to come through uncertainty affecting business and consumer confidence, and then business-investment and household-spending decisions, Boyton says. There isn't enough evidence in soft data to suggest downside risks to growth are crystallizing, he adds. (james.glynn@wsj.com; @JamesGlynnWSJ)

0258 GMT - RBC Capital Markets analysts say they are sanguine on the Australian mining sector. While commodities have been buffeted by tariff threats and soft economic data, fears of a major global slowdown are overdone, the analysts reckon. They revise their commodity forecasts, boosting their price estimates for gold but paring for metallurgical coal and lithium. "We forecast most commodity prices to be flattish/small declines in 2025," with the exception of precious metals, they say. RBC's key stock picks are Fortescue, Sandfire, Pilbara Minerals and IGO as well as Bellevue Gold, Westgold Resources, Regis Resources, South32, Mineral Resources and BHP. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0254 GMT - Iron ore rises in early Asian trading. Steel mills remain pessimistic about future market demand, but profits so far have rebounded significantly along with molten-iron production, analysts at Nanhua Futures write in a note. There is little room for further downside in prices, which are already at relatively low levels, and the market remains tight, with iron-ore port inventories destocking beyond seasonal levels and fundamentals staying strong, they say. Further upside will depend on market demand, they add. The most-traded iron-ore contract on the Dalian Commodity Exchange is 1.7% higher at 789.0 yuan a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0242 GMT - Palm oil prices fall in Asian trading amid continued slow physical demand, AmInvestment Bank says in a note. Palm oil market is expected to remain range-bound until mid-April before easing, helping to reduce palm oil's unusual premium over soyoil, it says. However, techinical analysis suggest CPO futures appears bullish, prices could hover around MYR4,480/ton-MYR4,570/ton, any price adjustment could offer opportunity for long position, it adds. AmInvestment Bank sees support for CPO prices at 4,430 ringgit/ton and resistance at 4,567 ringgit/ton. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for May delivery falls 22 ringgit to 4,517 ringgit/ton. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0240 GMT - The planned acquisition of Radius Recycling by a U.S. unit of Toyota Tsusho at a roughly 115% premium looks positive for ASX-listed metals recycler Sims, says Citi analyst Paul McTaggart. The Radius deal represents an EV/Ebitda multiple of 7.4X on 2027 estimates and a trailing three-year multiple of 8.3X, based on Visible Alpha consensus, says McTaggart. "We see this as a positive read-across for SGM with SGM trading on FY27 EV/Ebitda of 3.4X," he says. Citi has a neutral rating and A$15.50 target on Sims. The stock is up 4.7% at A$14.45. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

0211 GMT - Base metals are broadly higher in Asian trading. The three-month LME copper contract rises 0.3% to $9,810.00/ton, while that of nickel is 1.2% higher and aluminum is flat. Investors are gauging market appetite for new highs despite U.S. recession risks in focus amid renewed geopolitical tensions between Russia and Ukraine, the Sucden Financial team writes in a note. Meanwhile, the ongoing trade war is supporting aluminum prices, it adds. The lack of fundamental and technical triggers mean a significant push higher in prices will be challenging, Sucden Financial says. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

0205 GMT - Malaysia's equity market investors may remain defensive amid uncertainty over U.S. reciprocal tariff decisions on April 2, CIMB Securities analysts Ivy Ng Lee Fang and Lim Yue Jia say in a note. Large foreign outflows, driven by risk aversion and weak 4Q 2024 corporate earnings, have pressured the KLCI, they note. The index is 8.6% lower year-to-date at 1500.65. Foreign shareholding stands at 19.6% as of end-February, just above the record low of 19.4%, they flag. Catalysts such as easing trade tensions, a stronger ringgit, improved corporate earnings, or government initiatives are needed to drive a rebound, they add. CIMB highlights Genting Malaysia, Sime Darby, Malayan Banking, Gamuda, and RHB Bank as key picks for their large-cap status and high dividend yields. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0159 GMT - Chinese shares are higher in early trade, supported by consumer and insurance stocks. Investors await China's February economic data due Monday, including industrial output and retail sales. Gainers in the session include Yonghui Superstores, which is up 1.7%, Kweichow Moutai up 0.9%, China Life Insurance adding 0.6% and Ping An Insurance 1.1% higher. Steel stocks are broadly lower, with Baoshan Iron & Steel down 1.6%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index is 0.3% higher at 3369.64, the Shenzhen Composite Index is up 0.1% and the ChiNext Price Index rises 0.7%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0015 GMT - Australian lithium miner Liontown Resources delivered an in-line result, with revenue and cash flow largely pre-reported, say Barrenjoey analysts. First-half underlying Ebitda of A$66.0 million was well above of Barrenjoey and consensus estimates "given LTR took the approach of capitalizing all costs over and above revenue to effectively create a nil gross margin," the analysts say. Commercial production at the Kathleen Valley processing plant was declared effective Jan. 1, and capitalization of costs will stop from that date, they say. Depreciation was higher-than-anticipated, but net debt was as expected, say the analysts. They continue to expect Liontown will meet 2H FY25 guidance. Liontown is up 0.4% at A$0.6175. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2357 GMT - Macquarie upgrades a string of Australian mining stocks, citing a more-positive view on gold, iron ore and lithium-rich spodumene prices. It also downgrades a few--all coal-mining stocks, with the thermal-coal outlook having especially soured, according to Macquarie analysts. In a note, the analysts say South32 and BHP are Macquarie's top picks among Australia's diversified miners, and are preferred over Fortescue and Rio Tinto. Newmont is its large-cap gold pick and IGO its top bet for lithium, while Mineral Resources "offers high reward for risk," they say. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

2355 GMT - Gold edges lower in the early Asian session on a likely technical correction after front-month gold futures settled at a record high overnight. The precious metal is now looking increasingly likely to reach and possibly exceed $3,000/oz level in the coming sessions, says Fawad Razaqzada, market analyst at City Index and FOREX.com, in an email. The greenback continues to stay mostly under pressure and, with the U.S. government bond yields falling and stock markets volatile, it's a positive environment for the safe-haven asset, the analyst adds. Spot gold is 0.1% lower at $2,986.42/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

2334 GMT - While Boss Energy is raising its exposure to TSX-listed junior Laramide Resources at a relatively low cost, UBS analysts say they'd prefer the Australian uranium miner's focus remain on its flagship Honeymoon project. In a note, the analysts say they understand Boss's intentions in buying more of Laramide but remain cautious given a moratorium on uranium mining in Australia's Queensland state, where Laramide is developing the Westmoreland project. Boss Thursday said it agreed to buy 9% more of Laramide, to take its stake to more than 18%. Laramide "holds the view that uranium mining will again be allowed within the state in short order," say the analysts, while highlighting that uranium has not been produced in Queensland since 1982. UBS has a buy rating and A$3.20 target on Boss. The stock is down 0.5% at A$2.20. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Bestec Power Electronics Decides Against Paying Dividend for 2024
data.description:
Bestec Power Electronics will not distribute dividends for 2024 following a board resolution, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The power converters and power supplies manufacturer did not disclose further details regarding the decision.

data.title: SelfWealth Says Court Orders Shareholder Meeting to Vote on Proposed Acquisition by Syfe Group Unit
data.description:
SelfWealth said the Australian Federal Court ordered that the company hold a shareholder meeting to consider and vote on a resolution to approve the proposed acquisition by Svava, the holding company of the Syfe Group, through a scheme of arrangement, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

The court also made orders approving the company's distribution of an explanatory statement and notice of the scheme meeting to shareholders, the filing said.

The meeting is expected to be held on April 22, the filing added.

data.title: Shanghai tin prices jump after Alphamin halts mining in Congo
data.description:
Tin prices on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) advanced on Friday as Alphamin Resources halted mining in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC).

Alphamin Resources said on Thursday that activity at the Bisie tin mine in Congo's North Kivu had been paused "after insurgent militant groups have recently advanced westward in the direction of the mine's location in the DRC occupying the towns of Nyabiondo".

Shanghai's most active tin contract jumped 8.8% to 288,450 yuan ($40,212.16) a metric ton on Friday, as 0346 GMT, after hitting the upper limit of 10% earlier in the Asia morning trade session.

Benchmark three-month tin prices on the London Metals Exchange rose 0.6% to $36,110 a ton, retreating from its peak of $37,100 a ton hit in mid-2022 during intraday trading.

"Bisie tin mine is the world's third-largest tin mine, contributing to 6% of the world's tin ores in 2024," Wang Weiwei, an analyst at Yide Futures, said in a note.

Even as Myanmar's Wa state is looking into restarting its mining operations, Wang expects any significant increase in tin supply stemming from this area isn't expected to be evident until May 2025.

"We are closely monitoring the news about militancy in Congo, given its significant role as a global supplier of tin," a base metals trader said.

LME copper was up 0.4% at $9,817.5 a ton, LME aluminium was down 0.1% at $2,699.5 a ton, lead gained 0.3% to $2,079.5, and nickel rose 0.4% to $16,570.

SHFE copper climbed 0.7% to 80,150 yuan ($11,070.44)a metric ton, SHFE aluminium was down 0.1% to 20,960 yuan a ton, zinc added 0.2% to 24,120 yuan, lead jumped 0.4% to 17,640 yuan and nickel lost 0.2% to 133,300 yuan.

($1 = 7.2400 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: China Northern Rare Earth INvesting 73 Million Yuan in Hydrometallurgical, Materials Pilot Project
data.description:
China Northern Rare Earth Group High-Tech is investing 73.4 million yuan in a new hydrometallurgical and inorganic functional materials pilot base.

The project, with the construction site located in the company's smelting branch factory area, aims to enhance research and development innovation and technology conversion, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The facility will span 10,000 square meters and is set for completion in May 2026.

data.title: Market Chatter: Shanghai's STAR Market May Reopen Doors to Unprofitable Startups
data.description:
China's securities regulator is considering shifting its policy in a move that could ave the way for high-quality, yet unprofitable, startups to list on the Shanghai Stock Exchange's STAR Market, Yicai Global reported Thursday.

The China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) is reportedly exploring the reinstatement of the STAR Market's fifth set of listing standards. This move would allow companies with a market capitalization of at least 4 billion yuan to list even if they are not yet profitable.

CSRC Chairman Wu Qing previously signaled to commitment to supporting high-potential unprofitable startups by relaxing listing rules, according to the report.

The regulator reportedly held a meeting to discuss the proposed reinstatement.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: New Zealand Shares Notch Fourth Weekly Decline — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 rose 0.5% to 12266.25 on Friday, but it wasn't enough to prevent the index from recording a fourth consecutive weekly decline as global growth fears deepen. Investors have responded to tariff action among major economies by paring their equities holdings. Fisher & Paykel Healthcare, which has factories in Mexico that turn out products destined for the U.S., fell 0.2% to a new six-month low. Air New Zealand fell by 0.8%. Among stocks that are traditionally seen as defensive, Auckland International Airport rose by 0.6% following a late adjustment and Chorus gained 0.4%. A2 Milk was the day's top riser, up 7.0% as dairy prices continue to hover around record highs. The NZX-50 fell 1.1% across the week. (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Cardano (ADA) Struggle Persists—Is a Rebound Still Possible?
data.description:
Cardano price started a fresh decline below the $0.720 zone. ADA is correcting some losses and might face resistance near the $0.7750 level.

  • ADA price started a recovery wave from the $0.650 zone.
  • The price is trading below $0.720 and the 100-hourly simple moving average.
  • There is a connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $0.7050 on the hourly chart of the ADA/USD pair (data source from Kraken).
  • The pair could start another increase if it clears the $0.720 resistance zone.

Cardano Price Faces Hurdles

In the past few days, Cardano saw a bearish wave below the $0.7750 level, like Bitcoin and Ethereum. ADA declined below the $0.750 and $0.720 support levels.

Finally, it tested the $0.650 zone. A low was formed at $0.6495 and the price recently started a recovery wave. The price climbed above the $0.6950 and $0.70 level. The price even spiked above the 50% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $0.8170 swing high to the $0.6495 low.

Cardano price is now trading below $0.720 and the 100-hourly simple moving average. There is also a connecting bearish trend line forming with resistance at $0.7050 on the hourly chart of the ADA/USD pair.

On the upside, the price might face resistance near the $0.7050 zone. The first resistance is near $0.7520 or the 61.8% Fib retracement level of the downward move from the $0.8170 swing high to the $0.6495 low. The next key resistance might be $0.7750.

If there is a close above the $0.7750 resistance, the price could start a strong rally. In the stated case, the price could rise toward the $0.80 region. Any more gains might call for a move toward $0.850 in the near term.

Another Drop in ADA?

If Cardano’s price fails to climb above the $0.720 resistance level, it could start another decline. Immediate support on the downside is near the $0.6880 level.

The next major support is near the $0.650 level. A downside break below the $0.650 level could open the doors for a test of $0.6350. The next major support is near the $0.620 level where the bulls might emerge.

Technical Indicators

Hourly MACD – The MACD for ADA/USD is losing momentum in the bearish zone.

Hourly RSI (Relative Strength Index) – The RSI for ADA/USD is now below the 50 level.

Major Support Levels – $0.6880 and $0.6500.

Major Resistance Levels – $0.7520 and $0.7750.

data.title: Sinoma Science & Technology Cancels Issuance of Technology Innovation Bonds
data.description:
Sinoma Science & Technology cancelled the issuance of phase I of technology innovation corporate bonds, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen Stock Exchange.

The issuer, lead underwriter and investors agreed on the cancellation due to market volatility, the Chinese advanced materials manufacturer said.
